channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: FIFA prepares to give Saudi Arabia its biggest sports win yet as host of the 2034 World Cup
item.description: Saudi Arabia will be confirmed by FIFA as host of the men’s soccer 2034 World Cup on Wednesday, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: Graham Dunbar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike on home in northern Gaza kills 19 people, Palestinian medics say
item.description: An Israeli strike on a home sheltering displaced people in the northern Gaza Strip killed at least 19, Palestinian medical officials said Wednesday. Kamal Adwan Hospital said it received the bodies after the overnight strike in the town of Beit Lahiya.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill at least 29, Palestinian medics say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes on the Gaza Strip killed at least 29 people overnight and into Wednesday, including one that hit a home where displaced people were sheltering in the isolated north, killing 19, according to Palestinian medical officials.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's former defense minister attempted suicide after he was arrested over martial law probe
item.description: South Korea’s former defense minister, Kim Yong Hyun, attempted suicide while in detention over the president's martial law declaration, but officials were able to stop him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-defense chief in South Korea tried to kill himself after being arrested over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s previous defense minister was stopped from attempting suicide while in detention over last week’s martial law declaration, officials said Wednesday, as President Yoon Suk Yeol’s office resisted a police attempt to search the compound.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Ukrainian women are pulled alive from rubble hours after Russian missile strike
item.description: Rescue crews working through the night have pulled two women from rubble more than seven hours after a Russian missile struck a private medical clinic in a southern Ukraine city.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US Navy defeats Houthi attacks in Gulf of Aden for second time in weeks
item.description: U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale (DDG 106) and USS O’Kane (DDG 77) successfully defeated a range of Houthi-launched weapons while transiting the Gulf of Aden for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These Palestinians disappeared after encounters with Israeli troops in Gaza
item.description: Dozens of Palestinians are receiving help from an Israeli legal group in their search for family members who went missing after being separated by Israeli soldiers during raids and arrests in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Sarah El Deeb And Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu vows to challenge 'absurd' corruption charges as he takes the stand in his trial
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu promised defiantly on Tuesday to knock down corruption allegations against him as he began testifying in his long-running trial, becoming the first sitting Israeli leader to take the stand as a criminal defendant.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Humanitarian aid to northern Gaza mostly blocked for the last 2 months, UN says
item.description: Humanitarian aid to northern Gaza, where Israel launched a ground offensive on Oct. 6, has largely been blocked for the past 66 days.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gabbard meets with senators amid questions over Russia, Syria
item.description: Former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, met Tuesday with senators on Capitol Hill as she faces concerns from Republicans over her controversial positions sympathetic to U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The grinding war between Ukraine and its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump's inauguration, with President Joe Biden rushing out billions of dollars more in military aid before U.S. support for Kyiv’s defenses is thrown into question under the new administration.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, ILLIA NOVIKOV and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's ex-defense minister is formally arrested over brief imposition of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's previous defense minister was formally arrested Wednesday over his alleged collusion with President Yoon Suk Yeol and others in imposing martial law last week, as authorities investigate whether their acts amount to rebellion.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belarus has dozens of Russian nuclear weapons and is ready for its newest missile, its leader says
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Belarus' authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko said Tuesday his country is hosting dozens of Russian nuclear weapons and will prepare facilities for the planned deployment of Moscow's newest hypersonic ballistic missile.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes targets in Syria after Assad regime falls
item.description: Israeli forces carried out airstrikes on targets in Syria as Israel continues to take more aggressive action in the country following the collapse of Bashar Assad's regime over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Journalists' rights group says 104 media workers killed this year
item.description: More than half of the journalists and media workers killed perished during the Israel-Hamas war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Serbia's president says he won't flee his country like Syria's Assad did despite growing protests
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia's president accused Tuesday foreign intelligence services of trying to unseat him in the wake of spreading protests in the Balkan state and that he wouldn't flee the country like the ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: DUŠAN STOJANOVIĆ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US believes journalist Austin Tice is alive after disappearing in Syria in 2012, Biden says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Sunday that the U.S. government believes missing American journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared 12 years ago near the Syrian capital, is alive and that Washington is committed to bringing him home after Bashar Assad’s ouster from power in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: These are the countries with and without birthright citizenship
item.description: President-elect Trump doubled down on his vow to end birthright citizenship in his first postelection interview this weekend. “Yeah, absolutely,” Trump told NBC News’s Kristen Welker when asked whether he still plans to end birthright citizenship “on Day 1.” “We have to end it.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Pentagon chief: ‘Everybody expected’ stiffer resistance from Assad in Syria
item.description: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin said the international community was expecting Syrian President Bashar Assad to have a stronger response to the rebels that took control of Damascus over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour ends by shattering own record, grossing an estimated $2.2B, Pollstar says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Taylor Swift’s Eras Tour brought in $2.2 billion in its nearly two-year run, making it the highest-grossing tour of all time for a second year in a row, Pollstar estimates from data collected across 149 shows.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu to testify in corruption trial amid multiple conflicts
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will begin his witness testimony Tuesday for his ongoing trial on corruption and fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ousted Syrian leader Assad flees to Moscow after fall of Damascus, Russian state media say
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Ousted Syrian leader Bashar Assad fled to Moscow and received asylum from his longtime ally, Russian media said Sunday, hours after a stunning rebel advance seized control of Damascus and ended his family’s 50 years of iron rule.
item.dc:creator: ABDULRAHMAN SHAHEEN, SARAH EL DEEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Notre Dame opens to the public for first time since 2019 fire
item.description: Later on Sunday, an evening Mass will welcome members of the public who secured reservations last week.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: New reports claim UNRWA works with terrorists, teaches hate as agency hits back at critics
item.description: UNRWA is facing new allegations that its former and present leadership have met with Hamas and other terror-related groups. This comes as another NGO says agency-run schools continue to teach hate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's president avoids an impeachment attempt over short-lived martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's embattled President Yoon Suk Yeol avoided an opposition-led attempt to impeach him over his short-lived imposition of martial law, as most ruling party lawmakers boycotted a parliamentary vote Saturday to deny a two-thirds majority needed to suspend his presid
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against ‘Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: The nun, 57, is also well-known for her service in Milan's run-down districts. In February she was one of the recipients of the "Golden Panettone", an annual Milanese civic award.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump urges US to stay out of Syrian civil war, blaming Obama for failure as Islamists close in on capital
item.description: Amid the rapid offensive of Islamist rebels in their campaign to dislodge Syrian dictator Assad, President-elect Donal Trump has urged non-intervention by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president apologizes for declaring martial law ahead of impeachment vote
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized for declaring martial law earlier in the week, as he now prepares for a parliamentary vote on his impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against 'Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: As part of an investigation into the ‘Ndrangheta mafia in the city of Brescia, Italian police have arrested 25 people including a nun known for her prison work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation
